name: Power-law (sub-exponential) unperturbed tumor growth
description: Unperturbed tumor growth law dV/dt = a*V^p with p < 1. The specific growth rate a*V^(p-1) falls with size, so growth is SUB-EXPONENTIAL (slower than any exponential) but UNBOUNDED (no carrying capacity, unlike logistic/Gompertz/von Bertalanffy). Benzekry et al. (2014) found this the best-fitting unperturbed growth law across many experimental tumor datasets. Separable closed form V(t) = (V0^(1-p) + a*(1-p)*t)^(1/(1-p)); the p->1 limit recovers exponential growth (the dedicated growth_exponential kernel). Key consequence: assuming EXPONENTIAL growth (p=1) overestimates extrapolated tumor burden, since real growth decelerates. Illustrative values (a=0.16, p=0.75); review_status unverified.

extraction.tier_rationale: Established structural form; illustrative value (specific growth rate ~0.05/wk near 100 mm).
Benzekry-typical sub-exponential exponent (~0.7-0.8); p=1 recovers exponential.
